eeoc_gender_discrimination.description: 27.1% of EEOC charges are gender-based discrimination

hmda_black_denial_rate.description: Black applicant mortgage denial rate is 2.5x that of white applicants

nacac_legacy_advantage.description: Legacy admit rate is 3x non-legacy at selective institutions

hiring_name_callback_gap.description: White-sounding names receive 50% more callbacks than Black-sounding names

gender_pay_gap.description: Women earn 84 cents for every dollar earned by men

age_discrimination_charges.description: 21.1% of EEOC charges involve age discrimination

disparate_impact_80_rule.description: US 80% Rule: DI between 0.8-1.25 is considered fair

statistical_parity_threshold.description: Statistical parity difference should be within ±0.1

job.narrative: In hiring, AI systems have been shown to perpetuate gender and racial biases from historical training data. Amazon's resume-screening AI (2018) systematically downgraded women's applications.

loan.narrative: Lending algorithms have been found to charge higher interest rates to minority borrowers, even after controlling for creditworthiness. The Fair Lending Act requires equal access.
loan.key_legislation: Equal Credit Opportunity Act (1974), Fair Housing Act

college.narrative: College admissions algorithms can perpetuate socioeconomic inequality through proxies like zip code, legacy status, and extracurricular access.
college.key_legislation: Students for Fair Admissions v. Harvard (2023)
